remediation: Make SanctionedGitCommit.Commit's pathspec DIRECTORY-AWARE: commit the candidates that have a staged change AT or UNDER them (AtOrUnder: exact match OR startsWith candidate+'/'), and report the expanded staged files — so a directory candidate's files are committed, none left staged, while an operator's pre-staged file OUTSIDE the candidate set is still never swept in. Regression test: a .doti/core directory candidate commits skills.json + the template with nothing left staged, and an out-of-tree operator-staged file stays uncommitted.
summary: Regression from 035's pathspec-scoped commit: DotiReconcileCommit.TouchedPaths records DIRECTORY candidates (.doti/core, .doti/profiles, tools/sentrux...); git add stages their files individually, but SanctionedGitCommit's file-exact commit pathspec (mine ∩ StagedPaths) missed them, so a consumer update left managed source files (.doti/core/skills.json + templates) staged-but-uncommitted. Found verifying an ergon 0.18.3 update; the old whole-index commit had masked it.
